metadata.notes: Content overhaul — Wingspan teaching standard. Sources: official rulebook, Stonemaier FAQ, Dized interactive rules, BGG forums, community strategy guides.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Boards & Mats
Your table will have a shared Basecamp board at the center — this holds the card display and the glory track. Surrounding it are 20 hexagonal location tiles arranged in 3 regions: 6 South (face-up at game start), 7 Central, and 7 North (both face-down, waiting for you to explore them). Each player gets a mech mat that tracks your power, guile, workers, and — most importantly — your action token position.

#### Cards
The game uses a single shuffled deck of 123 uniquely illustrated cards. Every card you draw is one of 3 types:
- **Item cards** — give you an instant benefit when played, plus an ongoing ability. You can also tuck these under your mat as permanent upgrades.
- **Meteorite cards** — have a meld ability that grows more powerful each time you tuck another meteorite. Cascading bonuses are the name of the game here.
- **Quest cards** — require your mech to be at a specific location to solve. Solved quests tuck under your mat and dramatically boost your scoring.

You also start with a **character card** and a **companion card** in your hand — these are your opening engine.

#### Miniatures & Tokens
Each player gets a large mech miniature (snaps onto a silicone base) and 4 glory tokens (stars) that you'll place on the board via Boast to trigger the endgame. Your action token — a small cube — sits on your mech mat and controls which actions you perform each turn. You'll also have power and guile tracking tokens.

The shared supply includes 50 worker meeples in 5 colors, a cloth bag filled with 36 corruption tiles (more on those shortly — they're worth money), map tokens, and coins (limited supply).

#### Other Components
- 14 Automa cards (solo mode)
- 2 player aid cards per player
- Custom insert with setup tray

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Core Loop
subtopics.content:
#### Overview
Expeditions is a standalone sequel to Scythe, set in an alternate 1920s. A meteorite has crashed near the Tunguska River, scattering corruption across the wilderness. You pilot a mech into the unknown to explore locations, play cards to build your engine, gather resources, and vanquish corruption — which is a good thing, because corruption tokens are worth money at game end.

#### Your 4 Actions
Your mech mat has 4 action spaces: **Move**, **Play**, **Gather**, and **Refresh**. A small cube — your action token — sits on one of these spaces, and the space it covers is the one action you *cannot* take that turn. Here's how the cycle works:

**After a Refresh**: Your action token sits on the Refresh space. Shift it left into the Move/Play/Gather area — now all 3 of those actions are uncovered, and you perform all 3 in any order. This is your big turn.

**Subsequent turns**: Move the action token to cover exactly 1 of the 3 action spaces (Move, Play, or Gather). You perform the other 2 in any order. You must cover a *different* space than last turn — no repeating the same skip.

**Refresh turn**: When your hand runs low or your workers are all deployed, take a Refresh. Return all your active row cards to your hand and all workers to your mat. Place your action token on the Refresh space. Next turn, you'll get the big all-3-actions turn again.

The key insight: **Refreshing costs you a full turn of production.** The best players stretch their cycles as long as possible before Refreshing.

#### The Card Engine
Cards flow between 3 zones on your personal tableau:
- **Hand** (left of your mech mat) — cards waiting to be played
- **Active row** (right of your mech mat) — cards currently in play, generating abilities when you place workers on them
- **Tucked** (under your mech mat edges) — upgraded items, melded meteorites, solved quests. These score permanently and never leave.

#### Corruption: A Resource, Not a Punishment
This trips up new players: corruption is something you *want*. When you explore a face-down tile, corruption tokens get placed on it from the bag. You **vanquish** corruption by spending power and guile, and then you **collect** the token — it's worth **$2 at game end**. Even better, having **7+ corruption tokens** qualifies you for one of the glory categories you can Boast for. Think of corruption as treasure guarded by the wilderness.

#### Game End
When any player places their **4th glory token** via Boast, the endgame triggers. Every player (including the one who triggered it) takes exactly 1 final turn. Most money wins.

#### Checkpoint
Before your first turn, confirm: your mech is adjacent to Basecamp, you have your character + companion cards in hand, your action token is on Refresh, and your power and guile are both at zero. You're ready to explore.

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Setup
1. Place the Basecamp board centrally. Arrange the 6 South location tiles face-up per the layout diagram.
2. Shuffle Central (7) and North (7) tiles face-down in their spots. Place 1 map token on each face-down tile.
3. Shuffle all item, meteorite, and quest cards into one deck. Deal 5 cards face-up onto the Basecamp display.
4. Fill the corruption bag with all tokens except the 20-value token (set aside for Location 20).
5. Each player takes: mech mat + mech miniature, character card + companion card (into hand), 4 glory tokens, 1 action token (placed on Refresh space), power/guile tokens at zero.
6. Place all mechs adjacent to Basecamp. Randomly select first player.

With setup done, let's walk through each action in detail.

#### MOVE — Exploring the Map
Move your mech 1 to 3 spaces (Marsh Strider gets 1 to 4). You can pass through occupied spaces, but you cannot end on one. Once you leave Basecamp, you can never return to it.

The exciting part: **revealing tiles.** When you move onto a face-down tile, your movement ends immediately. Pick up the map token sitting on it. Flip the tile face-up. Then draw corruption tokens from the bag until their total value meets or exceeds the location's printed value — stack them on the location. Those corruption tokens are now waiting for someone to vanquish them.

Now that you know how to get around, let's look at what you do when you stop.

#### PLAY — Building Your Engine
Play exactly 1 card from your hand to the rightmost position in your active row. Then, in any order:
- **Gain the core value** (the number in the upper-left corner) — advance your power or guile track (max 10).
- **Activate the card's ability** — place a matching-color worker from your mat onto the card. Items give you instant and ongoing benefits. Quests let you Solve. Meteorites let you Meld.

Two rules to remember: worker color must match the card's requirement, and each card's ability activates at most once per turn.

Playing cards builds your active row — but eventually you'll need to harvest what's on the board. That's where Gather comes in.

#### GATHER — Collecting Location Benefits
Gain all exposed benefits at your mech's current location. Corruption tokens cover benefits underneath them until you vanquish the corruption away. You **must** gain all exposed benefits if possible — no cherry-picking.

Benefits vary by location and include: gaining a face-up card from the display, sweeping the display, gaining a worker, gaining a map token, playing a card, upgrading an item, melding a meteorite, boasting, and others. The location tile shows you exactly what's available.

#### REFRESH — Resetting Your Engine
When your hand is empty and your workers are all deployed, it's time to Refresh. Return all active row cards to your hand and all workers to your mat. Place your action token on the Refresh space.

Remember: Refreshing costs you an entire turn. Your next turn will be the powerful all-3-actions turn, but you've lost a full round of production in the meantime. Build your active row to 4-5 cards before resetting whenever possible.

Beyond the four core actions, several special abilities let you do more on your turns. Let's cover each one.

#### Vanquish — Clearing Corruption for Profit
Vanquishing isn't a standalone action — you trigger it through card abilities and location benefits. When you vanquish:
- Pay the cost on the topmost corruption token at your mech's location using power and/or guile.
- **Take the token** — it's yours now, worth **$2 at game end**.
- You may repeat to vanquish additional tokens at the same location.
- When you remove the final corruption from a location, the hidden benefit underneath becomes available for Gathering.

**Location 20** is special: its corruption requires BOTH 10 power AND 10 guile (not either/or). It's still only worth $2, but vanquishing it earns you a glory category.

Collecting corruption is **positive** — each token is $2, and having **7+ corruption tokens** qualifies you for one of the glory categories.

#### Solve — Completing Quests
Quests are your biggest scoring lever. To solve one:
- Your mech must be at the location matching the quest card.
- Pay the power/guile cost shown. Gain the benefit.
- Tuck the quest under the top edge of your mech mat. Max 4 solved quests.

Solving 3+ quests raises every glory star from $5 to $10. That's a $20 swing across 4 stars — don't skip quests.

#### Upgrade — Locking In Item Abilities
When you upgrade an item, you tuck it from your hand or active row under the right edge of your mech mat. The ongoing ability becomes permanently active (no worker needed to use it). Max 4 upgraded items. The coin value on the item also scores at game end.

#### Meld — Cascading Meteorite Bonuses
Tuck a meteorite under the bottom edge of your mech mat. Here's what makes melding special: you immediately gain the meld bonus of ALL currently melded meteorites, not just the new one. The 1st meld gives 1 bonus. The 2nd gives 2. The 3rd gives 3. The 4th triggers all 4 at once. Each successive meld grows dramatically more powerful.

Max 4 melded meteorites (Tatanka gets 5).

#### Boast — Placing Your Glory Stars
Boast is how you claim credit for your achievements and push toward the endgame. When you've achieved a glory category, you can place a glory star on it:
- **7 categories:** Quests (4 solved), Meteorites (4 melded), Items (4 upgraded), Location 20 (vanquished), Corruption (7+ tokens collected), Cards (8+ controlled), Workers/Maps (7+ workers OR 5+ map tokens).
- Multiple players can have stars on the same category.
- You need only **4 of 7** categories to trigger the endgame.

#### Mech Abilities
Each mech plays slightly differently:
- **Tatanka**: 5 slots for quests/melds/upgrades (instead of 4) — lets you go wider than anyone else
- **Marsh Strider**: Movement range 1-4 (instead of 1-3) — reach distant locations before opponents
- **Lumberjack**: Reduce first corruption cost per color by 1 — makes vanquishing cheaper and faster
- **Highlander**: Gain cards to hand instead of active row — more flexibility on when to play them
- **Odin's Wrath**: Trash a map token for 1 power, 1 guile, or $1 — converts exploration into fuel

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End Trigger
The moment any player places their **4th glory token** via Boast, the endgame begins. Every player (including the one who triggered it) takes exactly **1 final turn**. Use it wisely — a well-planned final turn can swing $10-15.

#### Final Scoring
Everything converts to money. Count up your total and the richest explorer wins.

| Source | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Coins | Face value |
| Upgraded items | Coin value printed on card |
| Corruption tokens | $2 each (including the 20-value token) |
| Glory stars | Variable — scales with quests solved |

**Glory Star Value by Quests Solved:**
- 0 quests: $5 per star
- 1 quest: $6 per star
- 2 quests: $8 per star
- 3+ quests: **$10 per star**

With 4 stars and 3 solved quests, your stars alone are worth $40. With 0 quests, those same stars are only $20. Solving quests is the single biggest scoring multiplier in the game — don't ignore them.

#### Tiebreaker
1. Sum of all glory category values (totals across every category, including those without stars).
2. If still tied: shared victory.

#### Typical Score Ranges
- First game: $35-50
- Experienced play: $55-75
- Highly optimized: $80+

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Minimize Your Refresh Turns
Every Refresh is a full turn where you produce nothing while everyone else advances. The best players squeeze 4-5 turns of productivity between each Refresh. Build your active row to 4-5 cards before resetting. If you're Refreshing every 2 turns, you're losing the game in tempo.

#### Solve 3 Quests — Your Top Priority
This is the single biggest scoring lever in Expeditions. Solving 3+ quests raises each glory star from $5 to $10. Across 4 stars, that's a **$20 swing** — the difference between winning and losing. Even solving 2 quests ($8/star) is a major improvement. Quests are not optional.

#### Plan Your Quest Setup Multiple Turns Ahead
Solving a quest requires 4 conditions to line up at once: (a) you have a quest card, (b) your mech is at the matching location, (c) you have enough power/guile to pay the cost, and (d) you play a card with a Solve ability that same turn. That alignment takes careful multi-turn planning — start positioning early.

#### Meld Meteorites for Cascading Value
Each time you meld a meteorite, you gain the meld bonus of ALL previously melded meteorites plus the new one. Your 4th meld triggers all 4 bonuses at once — that's 10 total bonus triggers across your 4 melds. Front-load your meteorite collection so the cascading starts compounding early.

#### Vanquish Corruption Aggressively
Corruption tokens are worth **$2 each** at game end, and collecting **7+ tokens** qualifies you for a glory category. That's potentially $14+ in tokens plus a glory star worth $5-10. Invest in power and guile early so you can vanquish efficiently as you explore. Leaving corruption on the board is leaving money on the table.

#### Leverage Your Mech's Unique Ability
- **Tatanka**: Go wide — your 5th slot for quests/melds/upgrades gives you more flexibility than anyone else at the table.
- **Marsh Strider**: Your extra movement range (1-4) lets you reach distant locations before opponents can get there.
- **Highlander**: Cards going to your hand instead of your active row gives you control over timing.
- **Lumberjack**: Your reduced corruption costs make you the most efficient vanquisher. Lean into the corruption glory category.
- **Odin's Wrath**: Convert excess map tokens into power, guile, or money — exploration fuels your engine directly.

#### Choose Your 4 Glory Paths Based on the Board
You need only 4 of the 7 categories. Read the board state, your card draws, and your opponents' progress. Pick the 4 categories that align with your current engine — don't chase all 7.

#### Watch for the Endgame Trigger
The game ends when anyone places their 4th glory star. If an opponent is sitting at 3 stars, you need to either accelerate your own Boasts or make sure your final turn scoring is locked in. Getting caught off-guard by a sudden endgame is one of the most common ways to lose.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Refreshing too often.** Every Refresh is a lost turn. Build larger active rows before resetting.
- **Ignoring quests.** Zero solved quests means your 4 stars are worth only $20 instead of $40. This is the biggest scoring mistake you can make.
- **Avoiding corruption.** Corruption tokens are $2 each and count toward a glory category. Vanquish them — don't leave free money on the board.
- **Spreading across all 7 glory categories.** Focus on 4 achievable categories rather than half-completing 6.
- **Forgetting worker color matching.** Your workers must match the card's color requirement to activate an ability. Plan your worker allocation before you play a card.
- **Skipping location benefits.** You MUST gain all exposed benefits when Gathering. Plan your movement so you land on locations where every exposed benefit helps you.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Expeditions

**Before You Start**: You have your mech adjacent to Basecamp, a character card and companion card in your hand, your action token sitting on Refresh, and power and guile both at zero. The 6 South locations are face-up around Basecamp; Central and North tiles are face-down with map tokens on them, waiting to be explored.

**Turn 1 — The Big Opening (All 3 Actions)**: Your action token starts on Refresh, so shift it left into the Move/Play/Gather area. All 3 actions are yours this turn. Move your mech to a nearby South location. Play your character card to your active row — gain its core value (advance power or guile) and activate its ability by placing a matching-color worker on it. Then Gather at your location to pick up an exposed benefit — maybe a face-up card from the display or a new worker.

Checkpoint: you should have 1 card in your active row, 1 worker deployed, and some power or guile on your tracker.

**Turn 2 — Choosing What to Skip (2 Actions)**: Now shift your action token to cover one of the three action spaces. Covering Move means you stay put and Play + Gather. Covering Gather means you Move + Play somewhere new. Covering Play means you Move + Gather without adding to your engine. Choose based on what your current location offers and what's in your hand.

**Turns 3-5 — Explore and Build**: Play item and meteorite cards to grow your active row. Move onto face-down tiles to explore them — you'll flip them face-up, gain their map tokens, and watch corruption tokens stack on the newly revealed locations. Start building your power and guile so you can vanquish that corruption for $2 per token.

**Mid-Game — Your Engine Running**: By now your active row should have 3-4 cards generating abilities each turn. When your workers are all placed and your hand is running low, take a Refresh turn — return everything to your hand and mat, then enjoy the all-3-actions turn that follows.

**Quest Solving**: When you have a quest card, position your mech at the matching location, build enough power/guile to pay the cost, and play a card with a Solve ability on your Play action. The quest tucks under your mat permanently. Aim for at least 3 solved quests — your glory star value jumps from $5 each to $10 each.

**Vanquishing Corruption**: When your mech is at a location with corruption tokens, use your card abilities to vanquish them. Pay the power/guile cost shown on the topmost token, then take it. Each token is worth $2 at game end. Clear all corruption from a location to unlock the hidden benefit underneath for future Gather actions.

**Endgame Push**: Once you've achieved 4 glory categories (for example: 4 solved quests, 4 upgraded items, 7+ corruption tokens, and 8+ cards controlled), find Boast benefits during your Gather actions and place your 4 stars. The game ends after everyone takes 1 final turn.

**Scoring**: Add up your coins + upgraded item values + $2 per corruption token + glory star values (scaled by quests solved). First game target: $40-55 is solid.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Action Token Optimization
The action token system is the core strategic puzzle in Expeditions. You get all 3 actions after a Refresh, then 2 actions per turn until your next Refresh. Every Refresh is a full turn of lost productivity — so stretching your cycles is everything.

- **Ideal cycle length: 4-5 turns per Refresh.** Build your active row to 4-5 cards before resetting. Shorter cycles waste Refresh turns; longer cycles leave your hand empty and workers stuck on cards.
- **Cover Move when your location has multiple benefits.** Staying put to Play + Gather maximizes a rich location.
- **Cover Gather when you need to reposition.** Move + Play lets you travel and build your engine at the same time.
- **Cover Play when a location has Boast or Vanquish benefits.** Move + Gather lets you reach a location and immediately collect.

#### Quest-First Engine Building
Solving 3+ quests makes every glory star worth $10 instead of $5. Across 4 stars, that's a $20 swing. Structure your entire early game around quest completion:

1. **Acquire quest cards early** from the Basecamp display or via Gather benefits.
2. **Plot your movement toward quest locations** while exploring nearby tiles along the way.
3. **Build power and guile to meet quest costs** through your character, companion, and item cards.
4. **Time your Play action** so you have a Solve ability ready the turn your mech reaches the quest location.

The payoff is enormous: 3 solved quests + 4 glory stars = $40 in stars alone.

#### Corruption as a Scoring Engine
Corruption tokens are worth **$2 each**, and collecting **7+ tokens** earns you a glory star. A player who vanquishes 8 corruption tokens scores $16 in tokens plus a star worth $5-10. That makes corruption one of the most efficient glory paths in the game.

- **Lumberjack** has a natural advantage here — reduced corruption costs mean faster, cheaper vanquishing.
- **Invest in both power AND guile.** Most corruption tokens accept either, but Location 20 requires both at max (10 power + 10 guile).
- **Explore aggressively** to spawn corruption on new tiles, then vanquish before opponents reach the same locations.

#### Meld Cascading
Meteorites cascade: the 1st meld gives 1 bonus, the 2nd gives 2, the 3rd gives 3, and the 4th gives all 4 at once. That's 10 total meld bonus triggers across 4 melds.

- **Front-load meteorite collection** so early melds start compounding immediately.
- **Pair meteorites with complementary bonuses** — a meld that grants power alongside one that grants guile accelerates your vanquishing.
- **Tatanka's 5th slot** for melds is especially powerful — 5 cascading melds produce 15 total bonus triggers.

#### Glory Path Selection
You need 4 of 7 categories. Here's how to evaluate which 4 fit your game:

- **Quests (4 solved):** Always pursue — the scoring multiplier affects ALL your stars. The single most impactful category.
- **Corruption (7+ tokens):** Strong with Lumberjack or aggressive exploration. Worth pursuing if you're already vanquishing.
- **Items (4 upgraded):** Natural byproduct of engine building. Easy to reach if you're drawing lots of items.
- **Meteorites (4 melded):** Cascading bonuses make this self-reinforcing. Especially strong with Tatanka.
- **Cards (8+ controlled):** Count your hand + active row + tucked cards. Often reachable passively by mid-game.
- **Workers/Maps (7+ workers OR 5+ maps):** Map tokens come from exploring face-down tiles; workers come from Gather benefits.
- **Location 20:** Requires maxing both power and guile. High cost, but you only need 1 vanquish to qualify.

#### Endgame Trigger Timing
The game ends when someone places their 4th star. Everyone — including the trigger player — gets exactly 1 final turn.

- **Trigger early when you're ahead in money.** If your engine generates more per turn than your opponents', ending the game locks in your advantage.
- **Delay by not Boasting** if you need 1-2 more turns to complete quests or vanquish high-value corruption.
- **Watch opponents at 3 stars.** If someone is one Boast away from triggering the end, make sure your final turn is already planned.

rulings: The action token stays in the Move/Play/Gather box once placed there. You must Refresh to return to all-3-actions mode.
'Gain 1 location benefit' means exactly 1 benefit icon, even if the location shows multiple icons.
A card's ability may only activate once per turn unless the card specifically states otherwise.
Adjacency is always relative to the mech's physical position on the map, not to cards in your active row.
'Gain the core value of all solved quests' includes the quest being solved in the same action.
You must gain ALL exposed benefits when Gathering if possible. You cannot selectively skip them.
Power and guile cap at 10. Effects that would push above 10 stop at the cap.
The 20-value corruption requires BOTH 10 power AND 10 guile (not either/or). It is still only worth $2 at game end.
Worker color must match the card's color requirement to activate an ability.
Gaining a Refresh benefit from a card is NOT the same as taking the Refresh action — your action token does NOT move to the Refresh space.
